parts.title: EdTech Coaches
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] class in [subject] that integrates [specific digital tool, e.g. coding or Google Classroom], with three activities and one goal from the national curriculum.
Give me five strategies to support teachers at a [primary school] in using [specific tool, e.g. Teams] in their teaching, with one practical idea for each.
Write a short tutorial for introducing [specific digital skill, e.g. coding or digital presentations] to a [grade X] class, with three steps and one example.
Suggest three ways to use [specific digital tool, e.g. Scratch or Padlet] to strengthen [specific goal, e.g. problem-solving or collaboration] in a [grade X] class.
Create a list of five quick tips for managing technical issues (e.g. [specific problem, e.g. wifi]) during a lesson at a [primary school].
Give me an example of a routine for planning a digital workshop for teachers at an [Upper Secondary school] on [specific topic, e.g. AI in teaching], with three steps.
Suggest three ways to integrate digital tools in a cross-curricular project for a [grade X] class between [subject 1] and [subject 2], with one idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class to use [specific tool, e.g. Canva] to create [specific output, e.g. a poster], with three steps.
Give me five ideas for using [specific tool, e.g. robots or apps] to engage a [grade X] class in [specific subject, e.g. mathematics].
Create a list of three short reflection questions for teachers at a [primary school] after a workshop on [specific digital topic, e.g. cybersecurity].
Suggest three ways to collaborate with school leadership at a [primary school] to implement [specific digital initiative, e.g. 1:1 devices], with one strategy for each.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's digital skills in [specific subject, e.g. Social Studies], with three criteria.
Write a list of five digital resources (e.g. websites or apps) for a [grade X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. language development].
Suggest three ways to handle students' uneven digital prior knowledge in a [grade X] class during a lesson with [specific tool], with one solution for each.
Create a template for a professional development session for teachers at a [primary school] on [specific tool or topic, e.g. coding], with three sections.
Give me five ideas for using [specific tool, e.g. VR or interactive whiteboards] to explore [specific subject, e.g. history] in a [grade X] class.
Suggest three ways to introduce [specific digital skill, e.g. online source criticism] to a [grade X] class, with one activity for each.
Write a short tutorial to support a teacher at a [primary school] in integrating [specific tool, e.g. Quizlet] into their teaching, with three steps.
Give me an example of a simple digital activity for a [grade X] class linked to [specific theme, e.g. sustainability], with three components.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with librarians or other resource staff at a [primary school] to promote [specific digital goal, e.g. reading].
Create a lesson idea for a [grade X] class in [subject] that uses [specific digital tool, e.g. Minecraft Education] to explore [specific goal, e.g. geometry], with three steps.
Give me five ways to help teachers at a [primary school] manage [specific digital issue, e.g. student logins], with one simple solution for each.
Write a short guide for introducing [specific digital skill, e.g. image editing] to a [grade X] class, with three steps and one practical task.
Suggest three ways to use [specific tool, e.g. Book Creator] to support [specific goal, e.g. writing development] in a [grade X] class, with one idea for each.
Create a list of five quick tips for ensuring cybersecurity when a [grade X] class uses [specific tool].
Give me an example of a routine for planning a digital theme day at a [lower secondary school] on [specific theme, e.g. source criticism], with three activities.
Suggest three ways to combine [specific tool, e.g. QR codes] with outdoor learning for a [grade X] class in [subject], with one task for each.
Write a short tutorial for teaching a [grade X] class to create a simple animation using [specific tool, e.g. Stop Motion], with three steps.
Give me five ideas for using [specific tool, e.g. Kahoot] to review [specific subject, e.g. history] with a [grade X] class.
Create a list of three short questions for evaluating a teacher's use of [specific digital tool] after a lesson at a [middle school].
Suggest three ways to collaborate with [specialist teachers] at a [primary school] to adapt [specific tool, e.g. text-to-speech] for students with [specific need].
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's ability to use [specific tool, e.g. Google Docs], with three criteria.
Write a list of five digital resources for a [grade X] class linked to [specific theme, e.g. sustainable development], with a short description of each.
Suggest three ways to introduce [specific digital skill, e.g. online collaboration] to a [grade X] class, with one group activity for each.
Create a template for a short workshop for teachers at a [lower secondary school] on [specific topic, e.g. flipped classroom], with three key components.
Give me five ideas for using [specific tool, e.g. green screen] to enhance [specific subject or unit, e.g. Science or optics] in a [grade X] class.
Suggest three ways to prepare a [grade X] class for a new digital tool (e.g. [specific tool]), with one introductory activity for each.
Write a short guide to support a teacher at a [primary school] in troubleshooting [specific technical problem, e.g. projector], with three concrete steps.
Give me an example of a digital project task for a [grade X] class in [subject] that uses [specific tool, e.g. Tinkercad], with three components.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with students at a [lower secondary school] to design a [specific digital project, e.g. school app], with one example for each.
Create a lesson activity for a [grade X] class in [subject] that uses [specific digital tool, e.g. Nearpod] to explore [specific theme, e.g. climate change], with three components.
Give me five strategies for overcoming teacher resistance to [specific tool or topic, e.g. coding] at a [primary school], with one positive idea for each.
Write a short tutorial for teaching a [grade X] class to create a simple website using [specific tool, e.g. Google Sites], with three steps and one example.
Suggest three ways to use [specific tool, e.g. Forms] to collect and analyse student responses in [subject] for a [grade X] class, with one idea for each.
Create a list of five quick solutions for managing [specific technical problem, e.g. slow internet connection] during a lesson at a [primary school].
Give me an example of a routine for introducing a new digital tool (e.g. [specific tool]) to teachers at a [primary school], with three steps.
Suggest three ways to use [specific tool, e.g. Flip] to strengthen [specific goal, e.g. oral presentation] in a [grade X] class in [subject].
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class the basics of [specific skill, e.g. computational thinking] using [specific tool, e.g. Code.org], with three steps.
Give me five ideas for using [specific tool, e.g. Miro] to promote group work in [subject] for a [grade X] class.
Create a list of three short reflection points for a [grade X] class after a lesson with [specific digital tool], to capture their experiences.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with school librarians at a [primary school] to integrate [specific tool, e.g. e-books] into teaching, with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple follow-up on a [grade X] class's use of [specific tool, e.g. OneNote], with three observation points.
Write a list of five digital tools or apps for a [grade X] class that support [specific subject, e.g. English], with a short description of each.
Suggest three ways to adapt [specific tool, e.g. interactive games] for students with [specific need, e.g. dyslexia] in a [grade X] class, with one solution for each.
Create a template for a 30-minute professional development session for teachers at a [lower secondary school] on [specific theme, e.g. blended learning], with three key steps.
Give me five ideas for using [specific tool, e.g. AR apps] to explore [specific subject or subject area, e.g. geography] in a [grade X] class.
Suggest three ways to introduce [specific digital skill, e.g. digital ethics] to a [grade X] class, with one discussion activity for each.
Write a short guide to help a teacher at a [primary school] create a digital portfolio using [specific tool, e.g. Seesaw], with three steps.
Give me an example of a digital collaborative task for a [grade X] class in [subject] that uses [specific tool, e.g. Jamboard], with three components.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with principals at a [primary school] to prioritise [specific digital goal, e.g. implementing a management tool], with one example for each.
